017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.parquet;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.marshall.parquet.ParquetSchemaElement.*;
022
023import java.io.*;
024import java.lang.reflect.*;
025import java.nio.charset.*;
026import java.util.*;
027
028import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
029import org.apache.juneau.marshall.collections.*;
030import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
031import org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream.*;
032
033/**
034 * Session for {@link ParquetParser}.
035 */
036@SuppressWarnings({
037   "java:S110",
038   "java:S115",
039   "java:S2583", // parquetDebug() is runtime-configurable via setDebugEnabled/-Djuneau.parquet.debug
040   "java:S3776",
041   "java:S6541", // Brain Method: Parquet parsing/serialization flows are inherently branchy
042   "java:S1192", // Duplicated literals (.list.element, root.list.element., value) are schema keys; constants would obscure
043   "resource"    // RecordReader returned by RecordAdapter is a Closeable owned by the caller; Eclipse JDT @Owning warning is by design.
044})
045public class ParquetParserSession extends InputStreamParserSession implements RecordReadable, ArrayRecordReadable {
046
047   private static final byte[] MAGIC = "PAR1".getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8);
048   private static final String ARG_ctx = "ctx";
049
050   /** Enable via {@link #setDebugEnabled} or -Djuneau.parquet.debug=true. */
051   private static boolean parquetDebug = "true".equals(System.getProperty("juneau.parquet.debug"));
052
053   /**
054    * Enable debug output for schema/column tracing.
055    *
056    * @param enabled <jk>true</jk> to enable debug logging to temp file.
057    */
058   public static void setDebugEnabled(boolean enabled) {
059      parquetDebug = enabled;
060   }
061
062   private static boolean parquetDebug() {
063      return parquetDebug;
064   }
065
066   private static void parquetDebugLog(String msg) {
067      try {
068         var f = java.nio.file.Paths.get(System.getProperty("java.io.tmpdir", "/tmp"), "juneau-parquet-debug.log");
069         java.nio.file.Files.writeString(f, "[Parquet DEBUG] " + msg + "\n",
070            java.nio.file.StandardOpenOption.CREATE, java.nio.file.StandardOpenOption.APPEND);
071      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") IOException ignored) {
072         // ignore
073      }
074   }
075
076   /**
077    * Builder for parser sessions.
078    */
079   public static class Builder extends InputStreamParserSession.Builder<Builder> {
080
081      private ParquetParser ctx;
082
083      /**
084       * Constructor.
085       *
086       * @param ctx The parser context.
087       */
088      protected Builder(ParquetParser ctx) {
089         super(assertArgNotNull(ARG_ctx, ctx));
090         this.ctx = ctx;
091      }
092
093      @Override
094      public ParquetParserSession build() {
095         return new ParquetParserSession(this);
096      }
097   }
098
099   /**
100    * Creates a new session builder.
101    *
102    * @param ctx The parser context.
103    * @return A new builder.
104    */
105   public static Builder create(ParquetParser ctx) {
106      return new Builder(assertArgNotNull(ARG_ctx, ctx));
107   }
108
109   private final ParquetParser ctx;
110
111   protected ParquetParserSession(Builder builder) {
112      super(builder);
113      ctx = builder.ctx;
114   }
115
116   /**
117    * Opens a whole-value pull-parser cursor over a Parquet document, bound to this live session.
118    * {@link RecordReader#read(Class) read(...)} delegates to the polymorphic
119    * {@link ParserSession#parse(Object, Class)} entry point.
120    *
121    * @param input The input.
122    * @return A new {@link RecordReader} cursor.
123    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred opening the underlying input.
124    */
125   @Override /* RecordReadable */
126   public RecordReader parseRecords(Object input) throws IOException {
127      return RecordAdapter.reader(this, input);
128   }
129
130   /**
131    * Buffered array-element {@link RecordReader} for Parquet, bound to this live session.  Calls
132    * {@code parse(input, List.class, Object.class)} once and iterates the result.
133    *
134    * <h5 class='section'>Why this stays buffered (not O(1) streaming):</h5>
135    * <p>
136    * Although a Parquet file is logically row-shaped, it is <b>not</b> a forward, element-at-a-time
137    * wire format.  Its metadata footer (schema + row-group/column-chunk offsets) lives at the
138    * <b>end</b> of the file, so a reader must seek to the tail before it can interpret any row, and
139    * values are stored <b>column-major</b> &mdash; reconstructing one logical record requires
140    * reading one page from every column chunk.  Both properties force whole-file (or at minimum
141    * whole-row-group) buffering; a true O(1)-in-rows cursor is not achievable over the current
142    * single-row-group in-memory decoder.  Left buffered by design.
143    *
144    * @param input The input.
145    * @return A buffered {@link RecordReader}.
146    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred reading the input.
147    */
148   @Override /* ArrayRecordReadable */
149   public RecordReader parseArrayRecords(Object input) throws IOException {
150      return RecordAdapter.arrayReader(this, input);
151   }
152
153   /**
154    * The Parquet record cursor is buffered/{@link RecordAdapter}-backed, not O(1) streaming.
155    *
156    * @return Always <jk>false</jk>.
157    */
158   @Override /* RecordReadable */
159   public boolean isRecordStreaming() { return false; }
160
161   /**
162    * The Parquet array-record cursor is buffered/{@link RecordAdapter}-backed, not O(1) streaming.
163    *
164    * <p>
165    * Parquet's end-of-file metadata footer and column-major value layout require whole-file
166    * buffering before any record can be reconstructed, so genuine element-at-a-time streaming is
167    * not feasible here; see {@link #parseArrayRecords(Object)} for the full rationale.
168    *
169    * @return Always <jk>false</jk>.
170    */
171   @Override /* ArrayRecordReadable */
172   public boolean isArrayRecordStreaming() { return false; }
173
174   @Override /* InputStreamParserSession */
175   public boolean hasNativeBytes() {
176      // Parquet's column reader has no native byte-array primitive type — it surfaces byte[] as a raw
177      // BYTE_ARRAY column (at BinaryFormat.NOT_SET; Bug #11 raw-bytes path) or as a UTF-8 string column
178      // (at any other BinaryFormat, after the BinarySwap reverses the configured text wire form).
179      return false;
180   }
181
182   @Override
183   @SuppressWarnings({
184      "unchecked" // Generic (T) casts required due to type erasure in doParse
185   })
186   protected <T> T doParse(ParserPipe pipe, ClassMeta<T> type) throws IOException, ParseException {
187      var bytes = readAllBytes(pipe);
188      if (bytes.length < 12)
189         throw new ParseException("Parquet file too small");
190      if (!startsWith(bytes, MAGIC, 0) || !startsWith(bytes, MAGIC, bytes.length - 4))
191         throw new ParseException("Invalid Parquet magic");
192      int footerLen = readLe4(bytes, bytes.length - 8);
193      int footerStart = bytes.length - 8 - footerLen;
194      if (footerStart < 4)
195         throw new ParseException("Invalid footer length");
196      var footer = Arrays.copyOfRange(bytes, footerStart, bytes.length - 8);
197      var meta = parseFileMetaData(footer);
198      if (meta.numRows() < 0 || meta.numRows() > MAX_NUM_ROWS)
199         throw new ParseException("Invalid numRows: {0}", meta.numRows());
200      ClassMeta<?> effectiveType = type;
201      if (type.isOptional())
202         effectiveType = type.getElementType();
203      var elementType = effectiveType.isCollection() ? effectiveType.getElementType() : effectiveType;
204      if (elementType == null || elementType.isObject())
205         elementType = ctx.getMarshallingContext().getClassMeta(Map.class);
206      // For scalar types, use Map so reassembleRows keeps raw map rows for unwrap logic
207      if (!effectiveType.isMap() && !effectiveType.isCollection() && !effectiveType.isArray()) {
208         var inner = effectiveType.inner();
209         if (inner != null && (inner.isPrimitive() || inner == String.class || inner == Boolean.class
210            || inner == Character.class || Number.class.isAssignableFrom(inner)))
211            elementType = ctx.getMarshallingContext().getClassMeta(Map.class);
212      }
213      var rows = readAllRows(bytes, meta, elementType, meta.schemaRepetition(), meta.rawByteArrayPaths(), meta.uuidPaths(), meta.columnLogical());
214      // Unwrap ValueHolder {value: X} when single row has "value" key and target expects scalar (not Map/List<Map>)
215      boolean targetWantsScalar = !type.isMap()
216         && !(type.isCollection() && type.getElementType() != null && type.getElementType().isMap())
217         && !(type.isArray() && type.getElementType() != null && type.getElementType().isMap());
218      if (rows.size() == 1 && rows.get(0) instanceof Map<?, ?> m && m.containsKey("value") && targetWantsScalar) {
219         var inner = m.get("value");
220         if (type.isOptional())
221            return (T)opt(convertToType(inner, type.getElementType()));
222         if (type.isArray()) {
223            var list = new ArrayList<>(List.of(inner));
224            return (T)convertToType(list, type);
225         }
226         if (type.isCollection()) {
227            Object coll = type.newInstance();
228            if (coll == null)
229               coll = new ArrayList<>();
230            ((Collection<Object>)coll).add(convertToType(prepareMapForBean(inner, type.getElementType()), type.getElementType()));
231            return (T)coll;
232         }
233         return (T)convertToType(prepareMapForBean(inner, type), type);
234      }
235      if (type.isMap()) {
236         // Unwrap ValueHolder {value: Map} when addRootType wrapped root Map (2.2)
237         if (rows.size() == 1 && rows.get(0) instanceof Map<?, ?> m && m.containsKey("value") && m.size() == 1) {
238            var inner = m.get("value");
239            if (inner instanceof Map<?, ?>)
240               return (T)convertToType(inner, type);
241         }
242         var keyType = type.getKeyType();
243         if (keyType != null && !keyType.isAssignableTo(String.class) && isKeyValuePairFormat(rows)) {
244            var valueType = type.getValueType();
245            if (valueType == null) valueType = ctx.getMarshallingContext().getClassMeta(Object.class);
246            var result = new LinkedHashMap<>();
247            for (var row : rows) {
248               var rowMap = (Map<?, ?>)row;
249               var rawKey = rowMap.get("key");
250               var k = (rawKey != null && ctx.nullKeyString.equals(String.valueOf(rawKey)))
251                  ? null
252                     : convertToType(rawKey, keyType);
253               var v = convertToType(rowMap.get("value"), valueType);
254               result.put(k, v);
255            }
256            return (T)convertToType(result, type);
257         }
258         if (rows.size() == 1 && rows.get(0) instanceof Map<?, ?> m) {
259            // Unwrap {root: {...}} from buildSchemaFromMap flat format
260            if (m.size() == 1 && m.containsKey("root") && m.get("root") instanceof Map<?, ?> inner)
261               return (T)convertToType(prepareMapForBean(inner, type), type);
262            return (T)convertToType(prepareMapForBean(m, type), type);
263         }
264      }
265      if (type.isOptional()) {
266         if (rows.isEmpty())
267            return (T)opte();
268         var inner = unwrapValueHolder(rows.get(0), type.getElementType());
269         return (T)opt(inner);
270      }
271      if (type.isArray()) {
272         var componentMeta = type.getElementType();
273         var unwrapped = new ArrayList<>();
274         for (var row : rows) {
275            var v = unwrapValueHolder(row, componentMeta);
276            unwrapped.add(v);
277         }
278         return (T)toArray(unwrapped, componentMeta);
279      }
280      if (type.isCollection()) {
281         var collElemType = type.getElementType();
282         var unwrapped = new ArrayList<>();
283         for (var row : rows) {
284            var v = unwrapValueHolder(row, collElemType);
285            unwrapped.add(v);
286         }
287         return (T)toCollection(unwrapped, type);
288      }
289      if (rows.size() == 1) {
290         var row0 = rows.get(0);
291         var unwrapped = unwrapValueHolder(row0, type);
292         return (T)unwrapped;
293      }
294      if (rows.isEmpty())
295         return null;
296      return (T)rows;
297   }
298
299   private static byte[] readAllBytes(ParserPipe pipe) throws IOException {
300      try (var is = pipe.getInputStream()) {
301         var baos = new java.io.ByteArrayOutputStream();
302         var buf = new byte[8192];
303         int n;
304         while ((n = is.read(buf)) >= 0)
305            baos.write(buf, 0, n);
306         return baos.toByteArray();
307      }
308   }
309
310   private static boolean startsWith(byte[] a, byte[] b, int off) {
311      for (int i = 0; i < b.length; i++)
312         if (a[off + i] != b[i]) return false;
313      return true;
314   }
315
316   private static int readLe4(byte[] b, int off) {
317      return (b[off] & 0xFF) | ((b[off + 1] & 0xFF) << 8) | ((b[off + 2] & 0xFF) << 16) | ((b[off + 3] & 0xFF) << 24);
318   }
319
320   private static final long MAX_NUM_ROWS = 10_000_000;
321   private static final long MAX_NUM_VALUES = 10_000_000;
322
323   /** Sentinel for a null intermediate OPTIONAL group at the given def level (GAP-14 multi-level nesting). */
324   private record GroupNull(int defLevel) {}
325
326   private record FileMeta(long numRows, List<RowGroupMeta> rowGroups, Map<String, Integer> schemaRepetition, Set<String> rawByteArrayPaths, Set<String> uuidPaths, Map<String, ColumnLogical> columnLogical) {}
327   private record RowGroupMeta(long numRows, List<ColumnChunkMeta> columns) {}
328   private record ColumnChunkMeta(int type, List<String> pathInSchema, int codec, long numValues, long dataPageOffset, long totalCompressedSize) {}
329
330   // Parquet page types (PageHeader.type).
331   private static final int PAGE_DATA = 0;
332   private static final int PAGE_DICTIONARY = 2;
333
334   // Parquet encodings (DataPageHeader.encoding).
335   private static final int ENCODING_PLAIN = 0;
336   private static final int ENCODING_PLAIN_DICTIONARY = 2;
337   private static final int ENCODING_RLE_DICTIONARY = 8;
338
339   /**
340    * One decoded Parquet page header plus the file offset at which its (possibly compressed) page
341    * body begins.  Covers both {@code DataPageHeader} (field 5) and {@code DictionaryPageHeader}
342    * (field 7); the unused sub-header's fields are left at their defaults.
343    *
344    * @param pageType {@code PageHeader.type} — {@link #PAGE_DATA}, {@link #PAGE_DICTIONARY}, etc.
345    * @param uncompressedSize Uncompressed page-body size in bytes.
346    * @param compressedSize Compressed page-body size in bytes (equals uncompressedSize when UNCOMPRESSED).
347    * @param numValues Value count carried by the page's own sub-header.
348    * @param encoding Data/dictionary page encoding ({@link #ENCODING_PLAIN}, {@link #ENCODING_RLE_DICTIONARY}, …).
349    * @param bodyStart File offset where the page body (compressed bytes) starts.
350    */
351   private record PageHeaderInfo(int pageType, int uncompressedSize, int compressedSize, int numValues, int encoding, int bodyStart) {}
352
353   private static final int MAX_PAGE_SIZE = 256 * 1024 * 1024;
354
355   /**
356    * Reads a single Parquet {@code PageHeader} Thrift struct starting at {@code off} in {@code fileBytes}.
357    *
358    * @param fileBytes The whole file.
359    * @param off Offset of the page header.
360    * @param columnPath Column path (for error messages only).
361    * @return The decoded header plus the offset where the page body begins.
362    * @throws ParseException If the header is malformed or declares out-of-range sizes.
363    * @throws IOException If the underlying Thrift decode fails.
364    */
365   @SuppressWarnings({
366      "resource" // bais is an in-memory ByteArrayInputStream; no OS resource to close.
367   })
368   private static PageHeaderInfo readPageHeader(byte[] fileBytes, int off, String columnPath) throws ParseException, IOException {
369      var bais = new ByteArrayInputStream(fileBytes, off, fileBytes.length - off);
370      var dec = new ThriftCompactDecoder(bais);
371      dec.readStructBegin();
372      int pageType = PAGE_DATA;
373      int uncompressedSize = 0;
374      int compressedSize = 0;
375      int numValues = 0;
376      int encoding = ENCODING_PLAIN;
377      ThriftCompactDecoder.FieldHeader fh;
378      while (!(fh = dec.readFieldHeader()).isStop) {
379         switch (fh.fieldId) {
380            case 1 -> pageType = dec.readI32();
381            case 2 -> uncompressedSize = dec.readI32();
382            case 3 -> compressedSize = dec.readI32();
383            case 5, 7 -> { // DataPageHeader (5) or DictionaryPageHeader (7): both carry num_values(1)+encoding(2).
384               dec.readStructBegin();
385               ThriftCompactDecoder.FieldHeader sub;
386               while (!(sub = dec.readFieldHeader()).isStop) {
387                  if (sub.fieldId == 1)
388                     numValues = dec.readI32();
389                  else if (sub.fieldId == 2)
390                     encoding = dec.readI32();
391                  else
392                     dec.skipField(sub.type);
393               }
394               dec.readStructEnd();
395            }
396            default -> dec.skipField(fh.type);
397         }
398      }
399      dec.readStructEnd();
400      int headerConsumed = (fileBytes.length - off) - bais.available();
401      int bodyStart = off + headerConsumed;
402      if (uncompressedSize < 0 || uncompressedSize > MAX_PAGE_SIZE
403         || compressedSize < 0 || compressedSize > MAX_PAGE_SIZE
404         || bodyStart + compressedSize > fileBytes.length)
405         throw new ParseException("Invalid page header for column ''{0}'': uncompressed=''{1}'', compressed=''{2}''",
406            columnPath, uncompressedSize, compressedSize);
407      if (numValues < 0 || numValues > MAX_NUM_VALUES)
408         throw new ParseException("Invalid page num_values for column ''{0}'': {1}", columnPath, numValues);
409      return new PageHeaderInfo(pageType, uncompressedSize, compressedSize, numValues, encoding, bodyStart);
410   }
411
412   /**
413    * Returns the offset of the first {@code DATA_PAGE} at or after {@code off}, skipping a leading
414    * {@code DICTIONARY_PAGE} if one is present.  Used by the repeated (list/map) chunk readers, which
415    * decode a single data page but must still tolerate a dictionary page in front of it.
416    */
417   private static int skipToDataPage(byte[] fileBytes, int off, String columnPath) throws ParseException, IOException {
418      var ph = readPageHeader(fileBytes, off, columnPath);
419      if (ph.pageType() == PAGE_DICTIONARY)
420         return ph.bodyStart() + ph.compressedSize();
421      return off;
422   }
423
424   /**
425    * Result of {@link #readSchema(ThriftCompactDecoder)}: the per-leaf repetition map plus the set of
426    * leaf paths whose physical type is {@code TYPE_BYTE_ARRAY} with no {@code convertedType} —
427    * i.e. raw {@code byte[]} columns emitted by {@code ParquetSchemaBuilder.addLeafSchema}'s
428    * {@code cm.isByteArray()} branch (Bug #11).  The Parquet file footer drops the
429    * {@code logicalType} discriminant ({@code ParquetSchemaElement.writeTo} omits field 7), so the
430    * parser uses "{@code TYPE_BYTE_ARRAY} with no {@code convertedType}" as the unique signal that a
431    * column should be reassembled back into {@code byte[]} instead of decoded as UTF-8 text.
432    *
433    * <p>
434    * {@code uuidPaths} carries the set of leaf paths that should be decoded as {@link UUID}. The decision
435    * prefers the explicit {@code LogicalType} discriminant (field 10) when present — a column is a UUID iff
436    * its logical type is UUID — and falls back to the legacy "{@code FIXED_LEN_BYTE_ARRAY} == UUID"
437    * physical-type heuristic when no logical type is recorded (backward compatibility with files written
438    * without the opt-in discriminator).
439    */
440   private record SchemaReadResult(Map<String, Integer> repetitions, Set<String> rawByteArrayPaths, Set<String> uuidPaths, Map<String, ColumnLogical> columnLogical) {}
441
442   /**
443    * Per-leaf logical-type metadata captured from the schema, used to decode native logical-type columns
444    * (GAP-9/10): the {@code convertedType} (parquet.thrift ConvertedType), DECIMAL {@code scale}/{@code precision},
445    * and the physical {@code type}.  All fields are best-effort: {@code convertedType} is null when absent.
446    */
447   record ColumnLogical(Integer convertedType, int scale, int precision) {}
448
449   private static FileMeta parseFileMetaData(byte[] footer) throws ParseException {
450      try {
451         var dec = new ThriftCompactDecoder(footer);
452         dec.readStructBegin();
453         long numRows = 0;
454         List<RowGroupMeta> rowGroups = null;
455         Map<String, Integer> schemaRepetition = Map.of();
456         Set<String> rawByteArrayPaths = Set.of();
457         Set<String> uuidPaths = Set.of();
458         Map<String, ColumnLogical> columnLogical = Map.of();
459         ThriftCompactDecoder.FieldHeader fh;
460         while (!(fh = dec.readFieldHeader()).isStop) {
461            switch (fh.fieldId) {
462               case 1 -> dec.readI32(); // version - consumed but not used
463               case 2 -> {
464                  var sr = readSchema(dec);
465                  schemaRepetition = sr.repetitions();
466                  rawByteArrayPaths = sr.rawByteArrayPaths();
467                  uuidPaths = sr.uuidPaths();
468                  columnLogical = sr.columnLogical();
469               }
470               case 3 -> numRows = dec.readI64();
471               case 4 -> rowGroups = readRowGroups(dec);
472               default -> dec.skipField(fh.type);
473            }
474         }
475         dec.readStructEnd();
476         return new FileMeta(numRows, rowGroups != null ? rowGroups : List.of(), schemaRepetition, rawByteArrayPaths, uuidPaths, columnLogical);
477      } catch (IOException e) {
478         throw new ParseException(e);
479      }
480   }
481
482   private record SchemaStackFrame(String name, int remaining) {}
483
484   private static SchemaReadResult readSchema(ThriftCompactDecoder dec) throws IOException {
485      var lh = dec.readListHeader();
486      var pathStack = new ArrayList<SchemaStackFrame>();
487      var result = new LinkedHashMap<String, Integer>();
488      var rawByteArrayPaths = new java.util.LinkedHashSet<String>();
489      var uuidPaths = new java.util.LinkedHashSet<String>();
490      var columnLogical = new LinkedHashMap<String, ColumnLogical>();
491      for (int i = 0; i < lh.size; i++) {
492         dec.readStructBegin();
493         Integer type = null;
494         Integer repetitionType = null;
495         String name = null;
496         Integer numChildren = null;
497         Integer convertedType = null;
498         Integer logicalType = null;
499         int scale = 0;
500         int precision = 0;
501         ThriftCompactDecoder.FieldHeader fh;
502         while (!(fh = dec.readFieldHeader()).isStop) {
503            switch (fh.fieldId) {
504               case 1 -> type = dec.readI32();
505               case 2 -> dec.readI32();
506               case 3 -> repetitionType = dec.readI32();
507               case 4 -> name = dec.readString();
508               case 5 -> numChildren = dec.readI32();
509               case 6 -> convertedType = dec.readI32();
510               case 7 -> scale = dec.readI32();      // SchemaElement.scale (DECIMAL)
511               case 8 -> precision = dec.readI32();  // SchemaElement.precision (DECIMAL)
512               case 10 -> logicalType = readLogicalTypeUnion(dec);
513               default -> dec.skipField(fh.type);
514            }
515         }
516         dec.readStructEnd();
517         if (name == null)
518            continue;
519         var parts = new ArrayList<String>();
520         for (var f : pathStack)
521            parts.add(f.name);
522         parts.add(name);
523         var path = String.join(".", parts);
524         if (numChildren != null && numChildren > 0) {
525            pathStack.add(new SchemaStackFrame(name, numChildren));
526         } else if (type != null) {
527            // Leaf element: record it and decrement parent's child count
528            int rep = repetitionType != null ? repetitionType : OPTIONAL;
529            result.put(path, rep);
530            // Capture logical-type metadata for native-type decode (GAP-9/10).  Only the DECIMAL and
531            // temporal converted types matter to the decoder; the UUID/string paths are handled separately.
532            if (convertedType != null)
533               columnLogical.put(path, new ColumnLogical(convertedType, scale, precision));
534            // Bug #11 fix: a TYPE_BYTE_ARRAY column with no convertedType uniquely identifies a
535            // raw byte[] column emitted by ParquetSchemaBuilder.addLeafSchema's cm.isByteArray()
536            // branch.  Every other writer site that emits TYPE_BYTE_ARRAY sets a discriminator
537            // (CONVERTED_UTF8 for strings/dates/durations/decimals, CONVERTED_ENUM for enums, or
538            // CONVERTED_TIMESTAMP_MILLIS).  Mirrors the Bug #7a UUID parse-back assumption
539            // (TYPE_FIXED_LEN_BYTE_ARRAY uniquely identifies UUIDs) at a different physical type.
540            if (type == TYPE_BYTE_ARRAY && convertedType == null)
541               rawByteArrayPaths.add(path);
542            // Prefer the explicit LogicalType discriminant (field 10) when present; otherwise fall back to
543            // the legacy "FIXED_LEN_BYTE_ARRAY == UUID" physical-type heuristic for backward compatibility
544            // with files written without the opt-in discriminator (work item 134).
545            if (type == TYPE_FIXED_LEN_BYTE_ARRAY)
546               uuidPaths.add(path);
547            if (parquetDebug())
548               parquetDebugLog("readSchema leaf: path=" + path + " name=" + name + " rep=" + rep
549                  + " convertedType=" + convertedType + " logicalType=" + logicalType + " pathStack=" + pathStack);
550            boolean moreToDecrement = true;
551            while (!pathStack.isEmpty() && moreToDecrement) {
552               var top = pathStack.get(pathStack.size() - 1);
553               var newRemaining = top.remaining - 1;
554               if (newRemaining <= 0)
555                  pathStack.remove(pathStack.size() - 1);
556               else {
557                  pathStack.set(pathStack.size() - 1, new SchemaStackFrame(top.name, newRemaining));
558                  moreToDecrement = false;
559               }
560            }
561         }
562      }
563      if (parquetDebug())
564         parquetDebugLog("readSchema result: " + result + " rawByteArrayPaths=" + rawByteArrayPaths + " uuidPaths=" + uuidPaths);
565      return new SchemaReadResult(result, rawByteArrayPaths, uuidPaths, columnLogical);
566   }
567
568   /**
569    * Reads a Parquet {@code LogicalType} union (parquet.thrift field 10) and returns the discriminant of
570    * the set union member (e.g. {@link ParquetSchemaElement#LOGICAL_TYPE_UUID} for {@code UUIDType}).
571    *
572    * <p>
573    * A Thrift union is encoded like a struct with exactly one field set; the field id is the union
574    * discriminant. Returns <jk>null</jk> if the union is empty (no member set).
575    */
576   private static Integer readLogicalTypeUnion(ThriftCompactDecoder dec) throws IOException {
577      dec.readStructBegin();
578      Integer discriminant = null;
579      ThriftCompactDecoder.FieldHeader fh;
580      while (!(fh = dec.readFieldHeader()).isStop) {
581         if (discriminant == null)
582            discriminant = fh.fieldId;
583         dec.skipField(fh.type);
584      }
585      dec.readStructEnd();
586      return discriminant;
587   }
588
589   private static void skipList(ThriftCompactDecoder dec) throws IOException {
590      var lh = dec.readListHeader();
591      for (int i = 0; i < lh.size; i++)
592         dec.skipField(lh.elemType);
593   }
594
595   private static List<RowGroupMeta> readRowGroups(ThriftCompactDecoder dec) throws IOException {
596      var lh = dec.readListHeader();
597      var list = new ArrayList<RowGroupMeta>(lh.size);
598      for (int i = 0; i < lh.size; i++) {
599         dec.readStructBegin();
600         List<ColumnChunkMeta> columns = null;
601         long groupNumRows = 0;
602         ThriftCompactDecoder.FieldHeader fh;
603         while (!(fh = dec.readFieldHeader()).isStop) {
604            if (fh.fieldId == 1)
605               columns = readColumnChunks(dec);
606            else if (fh.fieldId == 3)
607               groupNumRows = dec.readI64(); // RowGroup.num_rows
608            else
609               dec.skipField(fh.type);
610         }
611         dec.readStructEnd();
612         list.add(new RowGroupMeta(groupNumRows, columns != null ? columns : List.of()));
613      }
614      return list;
615   }
616
617   private static List<ColumnChunkMeta> readColumnChunks(ThriftCompactDecoder dec) throws IOException {
618      var lh = dec.readListHeader();
619      var list = new ArrayList<ColumnChunkMeta>(lh.size);
620      for (int i = 0; i < lh.size; i++) {
621         dec.readStructBegin();
622         ColumnChunkMeta meta = null;
623         ThriftCompactDecoder.FieldHeader fh;
624         while (!(fh = dec.readFieldHeader()).isStop) {
625            if (fh.fieldId == 2)
626               dec.readI64(); // file offset - consumed but not used
627            else if (fh.fieldId == 3)
628               meta = readColumnMetaData(dec);
629            else
630               dec.skipField(fh.type);
631         }
632         dec.readStructEnd();
633         if (meta != null)
634            list.add(new ColumnChunkMeta(meta.type(), meta.pathInSchema(), meta.codec(), meta.numValues(), meta.dataPageOffset(), meta.totalCompressedSize()));
635      }
636      return list;
637   }
638
639   private static ColumnChunkMeta readColumnMetaData(ThriftCompactDecoder dec) throws IOException {
640      dec.readStructBegin();
641      int type = 6;
642      List<String> pathInSchema = List.of();
643      int codec = 0;
644      long numValues = 0;
645      long totalCompressedSize = 0;
646      long dataPageOffset = 0;
647      ThriftCompactDecoder.FieldHeader fh;
648      while (!(fh = dec.readFieldHeader()).isStop) {
649         switch (fh.fieldId) {
650            case 1 -> type = dec.readI32();
651            case 2 -> skipList(dec);
652            case 3 -> pathInSchema = readStringList(dec);
653            case 4 -> codec = dec.readI32();
654            case 5 -> numValues = dec.readI64();
655            case 6 -> dec.readI64();
656            case 7 -> totalCompressedSize = dec.readI64();
657            case 9 -> dataPageOffset = dec.readI64();
658            default -> dec.skipField(fh.type);
659         }
660      }
661      dec.readStructEnd();
662      return new ColumnChunkMeta(type, pathInSchema, codec, numValues, dataPageOffset, totalCompressedSize);
663   }
664
665   private static List<String> readStringList(ThriftCompactDecoder dec) throws IOException {
666      var lh = dec.readListHeader();
667      var list = new ArrayList<String>(lh.size);
668      for (int i = 0; i < lh.size; i++)
669         list.add(dec.readString());
670      return list;
671   }
672
673   private List<?> readAllRows(byte[] fileBytes, FileMeta meta, ClassMeta<?> elementType, Map<String, Integer> schemaRepetition, Set<String> rawByteArrayPaths, Set<String> uuidPaths, Map<String, ColumnLogical> columnLogical) throws ParseException {
674      if (meta.numRows() == 0 || meta.rowGroups().isEmpty())
675         return List.of();
676      // Multi-row-group (GAP-2): read every row group and concatenate its reassembled rows in order.
677      // Each group declares its own num_rows; fall back to the file total only for a lone group whose
678      // per-group count wasn't recorded (older Juneau-written files).
679      var allRows = new ArrayList<Object>((int)Math.min(meta.numRows(), MAX_NUM_ROWS));
680      for (var group : meta.rowGroups()) {
681         int groupRows = (int)group.numRows();
682         allRows.addAll(readRowGroupRows(fileBytes, group, groupRows, elementType, schemaRepetition, rawByteArrayPaths, uuidPaths, columnLogical));
683      }
684      return allRows;
685   }
686
687   @SuppressWarnings({
688      "java:S107" // Parser-internal method threads decode state (column paths, schema repetition, logical types); parameter count is intentional.
689   })
690   private List<?> readRowGroupRows(byte[] fileBytes, RowGroupMeta group, int numRows, ClassMeta<?> elementType, Map<String, Integer> schemaRepetition, Set<String> rawByteArrayPaths, Set<String> uuidPaths, Map<String, ColumnLogical> columnLogical) throws ParseException {
691      var columnData = new LinkedHashMap<String, List<Object>>();
692      for (var cc : group.columns()) {
693         var path = String.join(".", cc.pathInSchema());
694         List<Object> values;
695         var trim = isTrimStrings();
696         if (isListColumnPath(path))
697            values = readListColumnChunk(fileBytes, cc, numRows, trim);
698         else if (isMapKeyValueColumnPath(path))
699            values = readMapKeyValueColumnChunk(fileBytes, cc, numRows, trim);
700         else
701            values = readColumnChunk(fileBytes, cc, numRows, schemaRepetition, rawByteArrayPaths, uuidPaths, columnLogical, trim);
702         columnData.put(path, values);
703      }
704      if (parquetDebug()) {
705         var summary = columnData.entrySet().stream()
706            .map(e -> e.getKey() + "->" + e.getValue())
707            .toList();
708         parquetDebugLog("readRowGroupRows: numRows=" + numRows + " columnData=" + summary);
709      }
710      return reassembleRows(columnData, numRows, elementType);
711   }
712
713   private static boolean isListColumnPath(String path) {
714      return path.endsWith(".list.element") || path.contains(".list.element.");
715   }
716
717   private static boolean isMapKeyValueColumnPath(String path) {
718      return path.contains(".key_value.key") || path.contains(".key_value.value");
719   }
720
721   private static String mapPropertyPath(String mapKeyValuePath) {
722      return mapKeyValuePath.substring(0, mapKeyValuePath.indexOf(".key_value."));
723   }
724
725   /** Returns true if rows are key-value pairs (Map with non-String keys format). */
726   private static boolean isKeyValuePairFormat(List<?> rows) {
727      if (e(rows))
728         return false;
729      var first = (Map<?,?>) rows.get(0);
730      return first.containsKey("key") && first.containsKey("value") && first.size() == 2;
731   }
732
733   /** Path relative to the row (bean). All schema leaf paths start with "root.". */
734   private static String rowRelativePath(String fullPath) {
735      return fullPath.substring("root.".length());
736   }
737
738   /** For list column path like "tags.list.element" or "members.list.element.name", returns the bean property name ("tags" or "members"). */
739   private static String listPropertyPath(String listColumnPath) {
740      int idx = listColumnPath.indexOf(".list.element");
741      return idx < 0 ? listColumnPath : listColumnPath.substring(0, idx);
742   }
743
744   private static List<Object> readListColumnChunk(byte[] fileBytes, ColumnChunkMeta cc, int numRows, boolean trimStrings) throws ParseException {
745      try {
746         var path = String.join(".", cc.pathInSchema());
747         var rowRelPath = rowRelativePath(path);
748         int listDepth = listDepth(rowRelPath);
749         int maxDef = 3 * listDepth;
750         int maxRep = listDepth;
751         int defBitWidth = 32 - Integer.numberOfLeadingZeros(maxDef);
752         int repBitWidth = 32 - Integer.numberOfLeadingZeros(maxRep);
753
754         if (cc.numValues() < 0 || cc.numValues() > MAX_NUM_VALUES)
755            throw new ParseException("Invalid numValues for column ''{0}'': {1}", String.join(".", cc.pathInSchema()), cc.numValues());
756         var codec = CompressionCodec.fromThrift(cc.codec());
757         // Skip a leading dictionary page if present, then read the single data page.  Repeated
758         // (list/map) columns carry rep+def levels with cross-page state; Juneau emits them as a single
759         // data page, and dictionary-encoded repeated columns are not produced here, so a single-data-page
760         // decode (via the shared page-header reader for Snappy + validation) is sufficient.
761         var chunkPath = String.join(".", cc.pathInSchema());
762         int dataPageOff = skipToDataPage(fileBytes, (int)cc.dataPageOffset(), chunkPath);
763         var ph = readPageHeader(fileBytes, dataPageOff, chunkPath);
764         var compressedData = Arrays.copyOfRange(fileBytes, ph.bodyStart(), ph.bodyStart() + ph.compressedSize());
765         var decompressed = codec.decompress(compressedData, ph.uncompressedSize());
766
767         int numValues = (int)cc.numValues();
768         int off2 = 0;
769         int repLen = (decompressed[off2] & 0xFF) | ((decompressed[off2 + 1] & 0xFF) << 8)
770            | ((decompressed[off2 + 2] & 0xFF) << 16) | ((decompressed[off2 + 3] & 0xFF) << 24);
771         off2 += 4 + repLen;
772         int defStart = off2;
773         int defLen = (decompressed[off2] & 0xFF) | ((decompressed[off2 + 1] & 0xFF) << 8)
774            | ((decompressed[off2 + 2] & 0xFF) << 16) | ((decompressed[off2 + 3] & 0xFF) << 24);
775         off2 += 4 + defLen;
776         var valueBytes = Arrays.copyOfRange(decompressed, off2, decompressed.length);
777         var repBytes = Arrays.copyOfRange(decompressed, 4, 4 + repLen);
778         var defBytes = Arrays.copyOfRange(decompressed, defStart + 4, defStart + 4 + defLen);
779         var repDecoder = new RleBitPackingDecoder(repBytes, repBitWidth);
780         var defDecoder = new RleBitPackingDecoder(defBytes, defBitWidth);
781         var valueReader = new ParquetColumnReader(valueBytes, numValues, 0);
782
783         var flattened = new ArrayList<Object[]>();
784         for (int i = 0; i < numValues; i++) {
785            int rep = repDecoder.readInt();
786            int def = defDecoder.readInt();
787            Object val = def >= maxDef ? readValue(valueReader, cc.type(), trimStrings) : null;
788            flattened.add(new Object[] { val, rep, def });
789         }
790
791         if (listDepth == 1)
792            return reconstructRowsFromListColumn(flattened, numRows, maxDef);
793         return reconstructNestedListColumn(flattened, numRows, listDepth, maxDef);
794      } catch (IOException e) {
795         throw new ParseException(e);
796      }
797   }
798
799   /**
800    * Reconstructs depth-N nested list columns (depth &ge; 2), replacing the former depth-2-only method.
801    *
802    * <p>This unified algorithm supports arbitrarily deep nesting (e.g. {@code boolean[][]},
803    * {@code List<boolean[]>}, {@code List<Long>[][]}, {@code List<Long[][]>}).
804    *
805    * <h5>Rep level semantics (depth=N, maxRep=N):</h5>
806    * <ul>
807    *   <li>rep=0 — new row; flush the entire stack and start fresh
808    *   <li>rep=k (0 &lt; k &lt; N) — new element at nesting level k; flush inner levels k+1..N
809    *   <li>rep=N — continue the innermost list (append another element)
810    * </ul>
811    *
812    * <h5>Def level semantics (maxDef = 3*N):</h5>
813    * <ul>
814    *   <li>def=2*(k-1) — null list at level k (1 &le; k &le; N)
815    *   <li>def=2*(k-1)+1 — empty list at level k (1 &le; k &le; N)
816    *   <li>def=maxDef-1 — leaf element is null
817    *   <li>def=maxDef — leaf element is present
818    * </ul>
819    *
820    * <p>The "stacks" array tracks the currently open list at each nesting level.
821    * {@code stacks[0]} is the outermost list (what will be placed in the row),
822    * {@code stacks[N-1]} is the innermost list being appended to.
823    */
824   @SuppressWarnings({
825      "unchecked" // Generic array creation for stacks.
826   })
827   private static List<Object> reconstructNestedListColumn(List<Object[]> flattened, int numRows, int depth, int maxDef) {
828      var result = new ArrayList<>(numRows);
829      var stacks = new ArrayList[depth];
830
831      for (var e : flattened) {
832         Object val = e[0];
833         int rep = (Integer)e[1];
834         int def = (Integer)e[2];
835
836         // Flush: close inner lists from the deepest level down to `rep`, adding each to its parent.
837         // For rep=0 this flushes everything including stacks[0] into result.
838         for (int k = depth - 1; k >= rep; k--) {
839            if (stacks[k] != null) {
840               if (k > 0)
841                  stacks[k - 1].add(stacks[k]);
842               else
843                  result.add(stacks[k]);
844               stacks[k] = null;
845            }
846         }
847
848         // Null at level 1 is the only case where def==0 AND rep==0; it was flushed above.
849         if (rep == 0 && def == 0) {
850            result.add(null);   // whole field is null
851         } else if (def <= 2 * (depth - 1) + 1) {
852            // Null or empty list at nesting level k (1-indexed): def=2*(k-1) → null; def=2*(k-1)+1 → empty.
853            int level = def / 2 + 1;
854            boolean isNull = (def % 2 == 0);
855            // Ensure stacks[start..level-2] exist (parents of the null/empty list).
856            int startK = (rep == 0) ? 0 : rep;
857            for (int k = startK; k < level - 1; k++)
858               if (stacks[k] == null) stacks[k] = new ArrayList<>();
859            if (level == 1) {
860               // Level-1 null is handled above (def==0); level-1 empty reaches here (def==1).
861               result.add(new ArrayList<>());
862            } else if (isNull) {
863               stacks[level - 2].add(null);   // null list at level k → parent gets null slot
864            } else {
865               stacks[level - 2].add(new ArrayList<>());  // empty list at level k
866            }
867         } else {
868            // Element (null if def < maxDef, present if def == maxDef).
869            if (rep < depth) {
870               // Initialize stacks from the first missing level down to the innermost.
871               int startK = (rep == 0) ? 0 : rep;
872               for (int k = startK; k < depth; k++)
873                  if (stacks[k] == null) stacks[k] = new ArrayList<>();
874            } else {
875               // rep == depth: continue innermost list (defensive init in case it's missing).
876               if (stacks[depth - 1] == null) stacks[depth - 1] = new ArrayList<>();
877            }
878            stacks[depth - 1].add(def >= maxDef ? val : null);
879         }
880      }
881
882      // Flush the final row's stacks.
883      for (int k = depth - 1; k >= 0; k--) {
884         if (stacks[k] != null) {
885            if (k > 0)
886               stacks[k - 1].add(stacks[k]);
887            else
888               result.add(stacks[k]);
889            stacks[k] = null;
890         }
891      }
892      while (result.size() < numRows)
893         result.add(new ArrayList<>());
894      return result;
895   }
896
897   private static int listDepth(String path) {
898      int depth = 0;
899      for (int idx = 0; (idx = path.indexOf(".list.element", idx)) >= 0; idx += 13)
900         depth++;
901      return depth;
902   }
903
904   private static final int MAP_MAX_DEF = 2;
905   private static final int MAP_MAX_REP = 1;
906
907   private static List<Object> readMapKeyValueColumnChunk(byte[] fileBytes, ColumnChunkMeta cc, int numRows, boolean trimStrings) throws ParseException {
908      try {
909         int maxDef = MAP_MAX_DEF;
910         int maxRep = MAP_MAX_REP;
911         int defBitWidth = 32 - Integer.numberOfLeadingZeros(maxDef);
912         int repBitWidth = 32 - Integer.numberOfLeadingZeros(maxRep);
913
914         if (cc.numValues() < 0 || cc.numValues() > MAX_NUM_VALUES)
915            throw new ParseException("Invalid numValues for column ''{0}'': {1}", String.join(".", cc.pathInSchema()), cc.numValues());
916         var codec = CompressionCodec.fromThrift(cc.codec());
917         // Skip a leading dictionary page if present, then read the single data page.  Repeated
918         // (list/map) columns carry rep+def levels with cross-page state; Juneau emits them as a single
919         // data page, and dictionary-encoded repeated columns are not produced here, so a single-data-page
920         // decode (via the shared page-header reader for Snappy + validation) is sufficient.
921         var chunkPath = String.join(".", cc.pathInSchema());
922         int dataPageOff = skipToDataPage(fileBytes, (int)cc.dataPageOffset(), chunkPath);
923         var ph = readPageHeader(fileBytes, dataPageOff, chunkPath);
924         var compressedData = Arrays.copyOfRange(fileBytes, ph.bodyStart(), ph.bodyStart() + ph.compressedSize());
925         var decompressed = codec.decompress(compressedData, ph.uncompressedSize());
926
927         int numValues = (int)cc.numValues();
928         int off2 = 0;
929         int repLen = (decompressed[off2] & 0xFF) | ((decompressed[off2 + 1] & 0xFF) << 8)
930            | ((decompressed[off2 + 2] & 0xFF) << 16) | ((decompressed[off2 + 3] & 0xFF) << 24);
931         off2 += 4 + repLen;
932         int defStart = off2;
933         int defLen = (decompressed[off2] & 0xFF) | ((decompressed[off2 + 1] & 0xFF) << 8)
934            | ((decompressed[off2 + 2] & 0xFF) << 16) | ((decompressed[off2 + 3] & 0xFF) << 24);
935         off2 += 4 + defLen;
936         var repBytes = Arrays.copyOfRange(decompressed, 4, 4 + repLen);
937         var defBytes = Arrays.copyOfRange(decompressed, defStart + 4, defStart + 4 + defLen);
938         var repDecoder = new RleBitPackingDecoder(repBytes, repBitWidth);
939         var defDecoder = new RleBitPackingDecoder(defBytes, defBitWidth);
940         var valueBytes = Arrays.copyOfRange(decompressed, off2, decompressed.length);
941         var valueReader = new ParquetColumnReader(valueBytes, numValues, 0);
942
943         var flattened = new ArrayList<Object[]>();
944         for (int i = 0; i < numValues; i++) {
945            int rep = repDecoder.readInt();
946            int def = defDecoder.readInt();
947            Object val = def >= maxDef ? readValue(valueReader, cc.type(), trimStrings) : null;
948            flattened.add(new Object[] { val, rep, def });
949         }
950
951         return reconstructRowsFromListColumn(flattened, numRows, maxDef);
952      } catch (IOException e) {
953         throw new ParseException(e);
954      }
955   }
956
957   private static List<Object> reconstructRowsFromListColumn(List<Object[]> flattened, int numRows, int maxDef) {
958      var result = new ArrayList<>(numRows);
959      var current = new ArrayList<>();
960      // def=0: list null; def=1: list present empty; def>=maxDef-1: element slot (value can be null)
961      int elemPresentDef = Math.max(0, maxDef - 1);
962      for (var e : flattened) {
963         Object val = e[0];
964         int rep = (Integer)e[1];
965         int def = (Integer)e[2];
966         if (rep == 0) {
967            if (!current.isEmpty()) {
968               result.add(new ArrayList<>(current));
969               current.clear();
970            }
971            if (def == 0)
972               result.add(null);
973            else if (def < elemPresentDef)
974               result.add(new ArrayList<>());
975            else if (def >= elemPresentDef)
976               current.add(val);
977         } else if (def >= elemPresentDef) {
978            current.add(val);
979         }
980      }
981      if (!current.isEmpty())
982         result.add(current);
983      while (result.size() < numRows)
984         result.add(List.of());
985      return result;
986   }
987
988   @SuppressWarnings({
989      "java:S107" // Parser-internal method threads decode state (column paths, schema repetition, logical types); parameter count is intentional.
990   })
991   private static List<Object> readColumnChunk(byte[] fileBytes, ColumnChunkMeta cc, int numRows, Map<String, Integer> schemaRepetition, Set<String> rawByteArrayPaths, Set<String> uuidPaths, Map<String, ColumnLogical> columnLogical, boolean trimStrings) throws ParseException {
992      try {
993         if (cc.numValues() < 0 || cc.numValues() > MAX_NUM_VALUES)
994            throw new ParseException("Invalid numValues for column ''{0}'': {1}", String.join(".", cc.pathInSchema()), cc.numValues());
995         var codec = CompressionCodec.fromThrift(cc.codec());
996         var path = String.join(".", cc.pathInSchema());
997         int rep = schemaRepetition.getOrDefault(path, OPTIONAL);
998         boolean isUnderListRoot = path.startsWith("root.list.element.");
999         boolean isPrimitiveType = cc.type() == TYPE_INT32 || cc.type() == TYPE_INT64 || cc.type() == TYPE_FLOAT || cc.type() == TYPE_DOUBLE || cc.type() == TYPE_BOOLEAN;
1000         // Max def level for a non-list/non-map leaf is the number of OPTIONAL groups enclosing it, i.e. the
1001         // rowRelative path segment count (GAP-14).  Single-segment columns keep the historical max=1.
1002         int rowRelSeg = rowRelativePath(path).split("\\.").length;
1003         int maxDefLevel = (rep == REQUIRED || (isUnderListRoot && isPrimitiveType)) ? 0 : rowRelSeg;
1004         int defBitWidth = maxDefLevel <= 0 ? 1 : 32 - Integer.numberOfLeadingZeros(maxDefLevel);
1005         boolean isRawByteArrayColumn = rawByteArrayPaths.contains(path);
1006         boolean isUuidColumn = uuidPaths.contains(path);
1007         var logical = columnLogical.get(path);
1008         if (parquetDebug())
1009            parquetDebugLog("readColumnChunk: path=" + path + " pathInSchema=" + cc.pathInSchema()
1010            + " rep=" + rep + " (REQ=" + REQUIRED + ") maxDefLevel=" + maxDefLevel + " type=" + cc.type()
1011            + " isPrimitive=" + isPrimitiveType + " isRawByteArrayColumn=" + isRawByteArrayColumn
1012            + " isUuidColumn=" + isUuidColumn + " logical=" + logical);
1013
1014         // Page loop (GAP-1): a column chunk may hold a leading dictionary page (GAP-3) followed by
1015         // one or more data pages.  Walk pages from dataPageOffset until the chunk's numValues
1016         // level/value entries are consumed.
1017         int valuesToRead = (int)Math.min(cc.numValues(), numRows);
1018         var values = new ArrayList<>();
1019         List<Object> dictionary = null;
1020         int pageOff = (int)cc.dataPageOffset();
1021         int consumed = 0;
1022         int pageGuard = 0;
1023         while (consumed < valuesToRead && pageOff < fileBytes.length) {
1024            if (++pageGuard > MAX_PAGES_PER_CHUNK)
1025               throw new ParseException("Column ''{0}'' exceeds the maximum of {1} pages per chunk", path, MAX_PAGES_PER_CHUNK);
1026            var ph = readPageHeader(fileBytes, pageOff, path);
1027            var compressedData = Arrays.copyOfRange(fileBytes, ph.bodyStart(), ph.bodyStart() + ph.compressedSize());
1028            var decompressed = codec.decompress(compressedData, ph.uncompressedSize());
1029            if (ph.pageType() == PAGE_DICTIONARY) {
1030               dictionary = readDictionaryPage(decompressed, ph.numValues(), cc.type(), trimStrings, isRawByteArrayColumn, isUuidColumn, logical);
1031            } else {
1032               int pageValues = Math.min(ph.numValues(), valuesToRead - consumed);
1033               boolean dictEncoded = ph.encoding() == ENCODING_PLAIN_DICTIONARY || ph.encoding() == ENCODING_RLE_DICTIONARY;
1034               readDataPageValues(decompressed, pageValues, maxDefLevel, defBitWidth, cc.type(), trimStrings,
1035                  isRawByteArrayColumn, isUuidColumn, logical, dictEncoded, dictionary, values);
1036               consumed += pageValues;
1037            }
1038            pageOff = ph.bodyStart() + ph.compressedSize();
1039         }
1040         if (parquetDebug())
1041            parquetDebugLog("readColumnChunk values: path=" + path + " values=" + values);
1042         return values;
1043      } catch (IOException e) {
1044         throw new ParseException(e);
1045      }
1046   }
1047
1048   private static final int MAX_PAGES_PER_CHUNK = 1_000_000;
1049
1050   /**
1051    * Decodes one PLAIN-or-dictionary-encoded data page into {@code values}, appending {@link GroupNull}
1052    * sentinels for null intermediate OPTIONAL groups (GAP-14).
1053    */
1054   @SuppressWarnings({
1055      "java:S107" // Parser-internal method threads decode state (page metrics, def levels, logical types, value sink); parameter count is intentional.
1056   })
1057   static void readDataPageValues(byte[] decompressed, int pageValues, int maxDefLevel, int defBitWidth,
1058         int type, boolean trimStrings, boolean isRawByteArrayColumn, boolean isUuidColumn, ColumnLogical logical,
1059         boolean dictEncoded, List<Object> dictionary, List<Object> values) throws IOException, ParseException {
1060      if (dictEncoded) {
1061         // Dictionary-encoded data page (GAP-3): def levels (if any) then a 1-byte bit-width followed by
1062         // RLE/bit-packed dictionary indices.  Nulls consume a def level but no index.
1063         readDictionaryEncodedPage(decompressed, pageValues, maxDefLevel, defBitWidth, dictionary, values);
1064         return;
1065      }
1066      var reader = new ParquetColumnReader(decompressed, pageValues, maxDefLevel, defBitWidth);
1067      while (reader.hasNext()) {
1068         var v = readValue(reader, type, trimStrings, isRawByteArrayColumn, isUuidColumn, logical);
1069         if (maxDefLevel >= 2 && reader.getDefLevel() < maxDefLevel - 1)
1070            values.add(new GroupNull(reader.getDefLevel()));
1071         else
1072            values.add(v);
1073      }
1074   }
1075
1076   /**
1077    * Decodes a {@code DICTIONARY_PAGE} body (PLAIN-encoded values, no def/rep levels) into an indexable
1078    * dictionary (GAP-3).
1079    *
1080    * @return The dictionary entries in index order.
1081    */
1082   static List<Object> readDictionaryPage(byte[] decompressed, int numDictValues, int type,
1083         boolean trimStrings, boolean isRawByteArrayColumn, boolean isUuidColumn, ColumnLogical logical) throws IOException {
1084      // A dictionary page is PLAIN-encoded with no definition levels (every entry present): maxDefLevel=0.
1085      var reader = new ParquetColumnReader(decompressed, numDictValues, 0);
1086      var dict = new ArrayList<>(numDictValues);
1087      while (reader.hasNext())
1088         dict.add(readValue(reader, type, trimStrings, isRawByteArrayColumn, isUuidColumn, logical));
1089      return dict;
1090   }
1091
1092   /**
1093    * Decodes a dictionary-encoded data page (GAP-3): optional RLE definition levels, then a leading
1094    * 1-byte bit-width followed by RLE/bit-packed dictionary indices.  Present values map their index
1095    * back through {@code dictionary}; nulls consume a definition level but no index.
1096    */
1097   // Package-private for focused unit testing of the dictionary-index decode path (GAP-3).
1098   static void readDictionaryEncodedPage(byte[] decompressed, int pageValues, int maxDefLevel, int defBitWidth,
1099         List<Object> dictionary, List<Object> values) throws ParseException {
1100      if (dictionary == null)
1101         throw new ParseException("Dictionary-encoded data page encountered with no preceding dictionary page");
1102      try {
1103         int off = 0;
1104         RleBitPackingDecoder defDecoder = null;
1105         if (maxDefLevel > 0 && decompressed.length >= 4) {
1106            int defLen = (decompressed[0] & 0xFF) | ((decompressed[1] & 0xFF) << 8)
1107               | ((decompressed[2] & 0xFF) << 16) | ((decompressed[3] & 0xFF) << 24);
1108            if (defLen >= 0 && defLen <= decompressed.length - 4) {
1109               off = 4;
1110               defDecoder = new RleBitPackingDecoder(decompressed, off, defLen, defBitWidth);
1111               off += defLen;
1112            }
1113         }
1114         // Dictionary indices section: 1-byte bit width, then RLE/bit-packed hybrid indices.
1115         int indexBitWidth = decompressed[off] & 0xFF;
1116         off++;
1117         var idxDecoder = new RleBitPackingDecoder(decompressed, off, decompressed.length - off, indexBitWidth);
1118         for (int i = 0; i < pageValues; i++) {
1119            int def = defDecoder != null ? defDecoder.readInt() : maxDefLevel;
1120            if (def < maxDefLevel) {
1121               if (maxDefLevel >= 2 && def < maxDefLevel - 1)
1122                  values.add(new GroupNull(def));
1123               else
1124                  values.add(null);
1125            } else {
1126               int idx = idxDecoder.readInt();
1127               if (idx >= dictionary.size())
1128                  throw new ParseException("Dictionary index {0} out of range [0,{1})", idx, dictionary.size());
1129               values.add(dictionary.get(idx));
1130            }
1131         }
1132      } catch (IOException e) {
1133         throw new ParseException(e);
1134      }
1135   }
1136
1137   private static Object readValue(ParquetColumnReader reader, int type, boolean trimStrings) throws IOException {
1138      // List/map element columns: no per-path discriminator is threaded, so keep the legacy
1139      // "FIXED_LEN_BYTE_ARRAY == UUID" assumption (isUuidColumn=true) to preserve existing round trips.
1140      return readValue(reader, type, trimStrings, false, true, null);
1141   }
1142
1143   // Package-private for focused unit testing of the external-read value-decode paths (INT96/DECIMAL/TIME/etc.).
1144   static Object readValue(ParquetColumnReader reader, int type, boolean trimStrings, boolean isRawByteArrayColumn, boolean isUuidColumn, ColumnLogical logical) throws IOException {
1145      reader.advance();
1146      if (reader.isNull())
1147         return null;
1148      // Native logical-type decode (GAP-9/10): when a column carries a DECIMAL/DATE/TIME/TIMESTAMP-micros
1149      // convertedType, decode to the matching value and surface a form the framework's coercion understands.
1150      // Read-side decode is always on; absent a logical convertedType the physical-type switch below applies.
1151      if (logical != null) {
1152         var nativeValue = readLogicalValue(reader, type, logical);
1153         if (nativeValue != NOT_LOGICAL)
1154            return nativeValue;
1155      }
1156      return switch (type) {
1157         case TYPE_BOOLEAN -> reader.readBoolean();
1158         case TYPE_INT32 -> reader.readInt32();
1159         case TYPE_INT64 -> reader.readInt64();
1160         // INT96 (GAP-8): legacy 12-byte timestamp (Impala/Hive/older Spark).  Decode to an Instant and
1161         // surface its ISO-8601 string so the framework's temporal coercion reconstructs the target type.
1162         // Read-only — Juneau never writes INT96.
1163         case TYPE_INT96 -> reader.readInt96AsInstant().toString();
1164         case TYPE_FLOAT -> reader.readFloat();
1165         case TYPE_DOUBLE -> reader.readDouble();
1166         case TYPE_BYTE_ARRAY -> {
1167            // Bug #11 fix: when the schema marks this column as a raw byte[] column
1168            // (TYPE_BYTE_ARRAY with no convertedType — see SchemaReadResult), surface the bytes
1169            // as a byte[] so the framework can hand them straight to the bean-property setter.
1170            // Otherwise read the bytes as UTF-8 for string / enum / date / duration / decimal
1171            // columns (which carry CONVERTED_UTF8 / CONVERTED_ENUM / CONVERTED_TIMESTAMP_MILLIS).
1172            if (isRawByteArrayColumn) {
1173               yield reader.readByteArray();
1174            }
1175            var s = reader.readByteArrayAsString();
1176            yield trimStrings ? s.trim() : s;
1177         }
1178         // TYPE_FIXED_LEN_BYTE_ARRAY is emitted by ParquetSchemaBuilder for UUID columns
1179         // (TYPE_FIXED_LEN_BYTE_ARRAY(16) / LOGICAL_TYPE_UUID).  Routing prefers the explicit
1180         // LogicalType discriminant when the writer emitted it (opt-in mode), and otherwise falls
1181         // back to the legacy "FLBA == UUID" physical-type assumption — both resolve to isUuidColumn
1182         // upstream (see readSchema).  Without the UUID conversion the parser would surface a raw
1183         // byte[16] into the row JsonMap and the framework's generic String/byte[]→UUID coercion
1184         // path can't reassemble it back to a UUID.
1185         case TYPE_FIXED_LEN_BYTE_ARRAY -> {
1186            var bytes = reader.readFixedLenByteArray(16);
1187            // A FLBA column with an explicit non-UUID logical type is surfaced as raw bytes rather than
1188            // misread as a UUID. Juneau's own writer never emits such a column today, so this is a
1189            // forward-compat guard for future FLBA logical types. // HTT
1190            yield isUuidColumn ? uuidFromFixedLenBytes(bytes) : bytes;
1191         }
1192         default -> {
1193            var s = reader.readByteArrayAsString();
1194            yield trimStrings ? s.trim() : s;
1195         }
1196      };
1197   }
1198
1199   /** Sentinel returned by {@link #readLogicalValue} when the column is not a recognized native logical type. */
1200   private static final Object NOT_LOGICAL = new Object();
1201
1202   /**
1203    * Decodes a native logical-type value (GAP-9/10) based on the column's {@code convertedType} and physical
1204    * type.  Returns {@link #NOT_LOGICAL} if the convertedType is not one this decoder handles (so the caller
1205    * falls back to the physical-type switch).  The reader has already been advanced and confirmed non-null.
1206    *
1207    * <ul>
1208    *    <li>DECIMAL (INT32/INT64) → {@link java.math.BigDecimal} with the schema scale.
1209    *    <li>DATE (INT32 days-since-epoch) → {@link java.time.LocalDate} ISO string.
1210    *    <li>TIME_MILLIS / TIME_MICROS → {@link java.time.LocalTime} ISO string.
1211    *    <li>TIMESTAMP_MICROS (INT64 micros-since-epoch) → {@link java.time.Instant} ISO string.
1212    * </ul>
1213    */
1214   // Package-private for focused unit testing of the native logical-type decode paths (GAP-9/10).
1215   static Object readLogicalValue(ParquetColumnReader reader, int type, ColumnLogical logical) throws IOException {
1216      int ct = logical.convertedType();
1217      switch (ct) {
1218         case CONVERTED_DECIMAL -> {
1219            // DECIMAL backed by INT32 or INT64: unscaled integer * 10^-scale.  BYTE_ARRAY/FLBA-backed
1220            // DECIMAL is left to the string path (not commonly emitted by Juneau).
1221            long unscaled;
1222            if (type == TYPE_INT32)
1223               unscaled = reader.readInt32();
1224            else if (type == TYPE_INT64)
1225               unscaled = reader.readInt64();
1226            else
1227               return NOT_LOGICAL;
1228            return java.math.BigDecimal.valueOf(unscaled, logical.scale());
1229         }
1230         case CONVERTED_DATE -> {
1231            if (type != TYPE_INT32)
1232               return NOT_LOGICAL;
1233            return java.time.LocalDate.ofEpochDay(reader.readInt32()).toString();
1234         }
1235         case CONVERTED_TIME_MILLIS -> {
1236            if (type != TYPE_INT32)
1237               return NOT_LOGICAL;
1238            return java.time.LocalTime.ofNanoOfDay(reader.readInt32() * 1_000_000L).toString();
1239         }
1240         case CONVERTED_TIME_MICROS -> {
1241            if (type != TYPE_INT64)
1242               return NOT_LOGICAL;
1243            return java.time.LocalTime.ofNanoOfDay(reader.readInt64() * 1_000L).toString();
1244         }
1245         case CONVERTED_TIMESTAMP_MICROS -> {
1246            if (type != TYPE_INT64)
1247               return NOT_LOGICAL;
1248            long micros = reader.readInt64();
1249            long secs = Math.floorDiv(micros, 1_000_000L);
1250            long microOfSec = Math.floorMod(micros, 1_000_000L);
1251            return java.time.Instant.ofEpochSecond(secs, microOfSec * 1_000L).toString();
1252         }
1253         default -> {
1254            return NOT_LOGICAL;
1255         }
1256      }
1257   }
1258
1259   /**
1260    * Reassembles a {@link UUID} from the 16-byte big-endian wire form written by
1261    * {@code ParquetSerializerSession#toFixedLenByteArray(UUID)}.
1262    *
1263    * <p>
1264    * Mirrors the serializer's write loop (most-significant 8 bytes then least-significant 8 bytes,
1265    * both big-endian) so the round-trip is bit-exact.  Falls back to {@code new UUID(0L, 0L)} when
1266    * the buffer is the wrong length — defensive guard for fuzzed input.
1267    */
1268   // Package-private for focused unit testing of the null/wrong-length guard.
1269   static UUID uuidFromFixedLenBytesForTest(byte[] b) {
1270      return uuidFromFixedLenBytes(b);
1271   }
1272
1273   private static UUID uuidFromFixedLenBytes(byte[] b) {
1274      if (b == null || b.length != 16)
1275         return new UUID(0L, 0L);
1276      long msb = 0L;
1277      long lsb = 0L;
1278      for (int i = 0; i < 8; i++)
1279         msb = (msb << 8) | (b[i] & 0xFFL);
1280      for (int i = 0; i < 8; i++)
1281         lsb = (lsb << 8) | (b[8 + i] & 0xFFL);
1282      return new UUID(msb, lsb);
1283   }
1284
1285   private List<Object> reassembleRows(Map<String, List<Object>> columnData, int numRows, ClassMeta<?> elementType) throws ParseException {
1286      var result = new ArrayList<>(numRows);
1287      var listBeanColumns = groupListBeanColumns(columnData.keySet(), elementType);
1288      var mapColumns = groupMapColumns(columnData.keySet());
1289      for (int i = 0; i < numRows; i++) {
1290         var row = new JsonMap();
1291         for (var e : columnData.entrySet()) {
1292            var fullPath = e.getKey();
1293            var rowRelPath = rowRelativePath(fullPath);
1294            var listProp = listPropertyPath(rowRelPath);
1295            if (listBeanColumns.containsKey(listProp) || isMapKeyValueColumnPath(rowRelPath))
1296               continue;
1297            var values = e.getValue();
1298            var v = values.get(i);
1299            if (v instanceof GroupNull gn) {
1300               // Null intermediate group: set the prefix path (up to the null level) to null without
1301               // synthesizing the deeper map structure (GAP-14).
1302               var parts = rowRelPath.split("\\.");
1303               var lvl = Math.min(gn.defLevel(), parts.length - 1);
1304               var prefix = String.join(".", Arrays.copyOfRange(parts, 0, lvl + 1));
1305               if (isTrimStrings())
1306                  prefix = prefix.trim();
1307               setByPath(row, prefix, null);
1308            } else {
1309               var path = isListColumnPath(fullPath) ? listProp : rowRelPath;
1310               if (isTrimStrings())
1311                  path = path.trim();
1312               setByPath(row, path, v);
1313            }
1314         }
1315         for (var e : listBeanColumns.entrySet()) {
1316            var listProp = e.getKey();
1317            var columnsInGroup = e.getValue();
1318            var mergedList = mergeListBeanColumns(columnsInGroup, columnData, i);
1319            setByPath(row, listProp, mergedList);
1320         }
1321         for (var e : mapColumns.entrySet()) {
1322            var mapProp = e.getKey();
1323            var columnsInGroup = e.getValue();
1324            var mergedMap = mergeMapColumns(columnsInGroup, columnData, i);
1325            setByPath(row, mapProp, mergedMap);
1326         }
1327         replaceNullKeySentinel(row);
1328         if (elementType.isBean())
1329            collapseOptionalWrappers(row, elementType);
1330         Object rowOrBean = elementType.isBean() ? convertToType(prepareMapForBean(row, elementType), elementType) : row;
1331         result.add(rowOrBean);
1332      }
1333      return result;
1334   }
1335
1336   private static Map<String, List<ListColumnInfo>> groupListBeanColumns(Set<String> columnPaths, ClassMeta<?> elementType) {
1337      var groups = new LinkedHashMap<String, List<ListColumnInfo>>();
1338      if (!elementType.isBean())
1339         return groups;
1340      var bm = elementType.getBeanMeta();
1341      for (var fullPath : columnPaths) {
1342         if (!isListColumnPath(fullPath))
1343            continue;
1344         var rowRelPath = rowRelativePath(fullPath);
1345         var listProp = listPropertyPath(rowRelPath);
1346         var suffix = ".list.element.";
1347         var suffixStart = listProp.length() + suffix.length();
1348         var pMeta = rowRelPath.length() > suffixStart ? bm.getPropertyMeta(listProp) : null;
1349         var propClassMeta = pMeta != null ? (ClassMeta<?>) pMeta.getBeanInfo() : null;
1350         var elemType = propClassMeta != null && (propClassMeta.isCollection() || propClassMeta.isArray()) ? propClassMeta.getElementType() : null;
1351         if (elemType != null && elemType.isBean()) {
1352            var elementProp = rowRelPath.substring(suffixStart);
1353            groups.computeIfAbsent(listProp, k -> new ArrayList<>()).add(new ListColumnInfo(fullPath, elementProp, elemType));
1354         }
1355      }
1356      return groups;
1357   }
1358
1359   private record ListColumnInfo(String fullPath, String elementProp, ClassMeta<?> elementType) {}
1360
1361   private record MapColumnInfo(String keyColumnPath, String valueColumnPath) {}
1362
1363   private static Map<String, MapColumnInfo> groupMapColumns(Set<String> columnPaths) {
1364      var groups = new LinkedHashMap<String, MapColumnInfo>();
1365      for (var fullPath : columnPaths) {
1366         var rowRelPath = rowRelativePath(fullPath);
1367         if (!isMapKeyValueColumnPath(rowRelPath))
1368            continue;
1369         var prop = mapPropertyPath(rowRelPath);
1370         if (rowRelPath.endsWith(".key_value.key"))
1371            groups.put(prop, new MapColumnInfo(fullPath, null));
1372         else if (rowRelPath.endsWith(".key_value.value")) {
1373            var prev = groups.get(prop);
1374            groups.put(prop, new MapColumnInfo(prev.keyColumnPath(), fullPath));
1375         }
1376      }
1377      return groups;
1378   }
1379
1380   private Map<Object, Object> mergeMapColumns(MapColumnInfo info, Map<String, List<Object>> columnData, int rowIndex) throws ParseException {
1381      var keyList = (List<?>) columnData.get(info.keyColumnPath()).get(rowIndex);
1382      if (keyList.isEmpty())
1383         return new LinkedHashMap<>();
1384      var valueList = (List<?>) columnData.get(info.valueColumnPath()).get(rowIndex);
1385      var result = new LinkedHashMap<>();
1386      var valueIter = valueList.iterator();
1387      for (var k : keyList) {
1388         var v = valueIter.next();
1389         Object key = ctx.nullKeyString.equals(String.valueOf(k)) ? null : k;
1390         result.put(key, v);
1391      }
1392      return result;
1393   }
1394
1395   private List<Object> mergeListBeanColumns(List<ListColumnInfo> columnsInGroup, Map<String, List<Object>> columnData,
1396      int rowIndex) throws ParseException {
1397      var elemType = columnsInGroup.get(0).elementType();
1398      var firstList = (List<?>) columnData.get(columnsInGroup.get(0).fullPath()).get(rowIndex);
1399      int listSize = firstList.size();
1400      var result = new ArrayList<>(listSize);
1401      for (int j = 0; j < listSize; j++) {
1402         var elemMap = new JsonMap();
1403         for (var col : columnsInGroup) {
1404            var rowList = (List<?>) columnData.get(col.fullPath()).get(rowIndex);
1405            elemMap.put(col.elementProp(), rowList.get(j));
1406         }
1407         result.add(convertToType(prepareMapForBean(elemMap, elemType), elemType));
1408      }
1409      return result;
1410   }
1411
1412   /**
1413    * Replaces keys equal to {@link ParquetParser#nullKeyString} (default <js>"&lt;NULL&gt;"</js>)
1414    * with actual <jk>null</jk> in maps. Enables round-trip of maps with null keys in flat (column-per-key) format.
1415    */
1416   @SuppressWarnings({
1417      "unchecked" // Cast is safe: Parquet schema type is verified at parse time.
1418   })
1419   private void replaceNullKeySentinel(Object obj) {
1420      if (obj == null)
1421         return;
1422      if (obj instanceof Map<?, ?> m) {
1423         if (m.containsKey(ctx.nullKeyString)) {
1424            var val = m.get(ctx.nullKeyString);
1425            ((Map<Object, Object>)m).remove(ctx.nullKeyString);
1426            ((Map<Object, Object>)m).put(null, val);
1427         }
1428         for (var v : m.values())
1429            replaceNullKeySentinel(v);
1430      } else if (obj instanceof List<?> list) {
1431         for (var e : list)
1432            replaceNullKeySentinel(e);
1433      }
1434   }
1435
1436   /** Collapses Parquet optional-group {value: X} wrappers so MarshallingSession receives unwrapped values for Optional properties. */
1437   @SuppressWarnings({
1438      "unchecked" // (Map<String,Object>) cast for mutating optional-group structure
1439   })
1440   private void collapseOptionalWrappers(Map<?,?> row, ClassMeta<?> elementType) {
1441      var bm = elementType.getBeanMeta();
1442      boolean hasOptional = bm.getProperties().values().stream().anyMatch(p -> p.getBeanInfo() != null && p.getBeanInfo().isOptional());
1443      if (!hasOptional)
1444         return;
1445      for (var pMeta : bm.getProperties().values()) {
1446         var name = pMeta.getName();
1447         var val = row.get(name);
1448         var propType = (ClassMeta<?>) pMeta.getBeanInfo();
1449         if (val == null)
1450            continue;
1451         if (propType.isOptional() && val instanceof Map<?,?> m) {
1452            var elemType = propType.getElementType();
1453            boolean absent = isAbsentOptionalMap(m, elemType);
1454            // Detect absent inner Optional: all-null map, empty map, or nested {value:{all-null}} structure.
1455            // Use null so MarshallingSession's null→Optional<Optional<X>> conversion produces Optional.of(Optional.empty()).
1456            if (absent) {
1457               ((Map<String,Object>)row).put(name, null);
1458            } else {
1459               var inner = m.get("value");
1460               var collapsed = collapseOptionalValue(inner, elemType);
1461               ((Map<String,Object>)row).put(name, collapsed);
1462            }
1463         } else if (propType.isBean() && val instanceof Map<?,?> m2)
1464            collapseOptionalWrappers(m2, propType);
1465      }
1466   }
1467
1468   /**
1469    * Returns true if the map represents an absent Optional value that should be treated as null,
1470    * allowing MarshallingSession's null→Optional<Optional<X>> conversion to produce Optional.of(Optional.empty()).
1471    *
1472    * <p>Absent patterns:
1473    * <ul>
1474    *   <li>Empty map {}
1475    *   <li>Map whose values are all null (e.g. {value: null})
1476    *   <li>Map {value: M} where M is itself absent (recursively): handles Optional<Optional<X>>
1477    *   <li>Map {value: M} where M has all null leaves (bean group for absent inner type)
1478    * </ul>
1479    */
1480   private boolean isAbsentOptionalMap(Map<?,?> m, ClassMeta<?> elemType) {
1481      if (m.values().stream().allMatch(v -> v == null))
1482         return true;
1483      var inner = m.get("value");
1484      if (inner instanceof Map<?,?> innerMap) {
1485         if (elemType.isOptional())
1486            return isAbsentOptionalMap(innerMap, elemType.getElementType());
1487         // For bean (non-Optional) inner type: check if all nested leaf values are null (absent bean group)
1488         return allNullLeaves(innerMap);
1489      }
1490      return false;
1491   }
1492
1493   /** Returns true if all leaf values in the nested map structure are null. */
1494   private boolean allNullLeaves(Object obj) {
1495      if (obj == null)
1496         return true;
1497      if (!(obj instanceof Map<?,?> m))
1498         return false;
1499      return m.values().stream().allMatch(this::allNullLeaves);
1500   }
1501
1502   /** Recursively unwraps {value: X} when target is Optional; collapses nested beans. */
1503   private Object collapseOptionalValue(Object val, ClassMeta<?> targetType) {
1504      if (targetType.isOptional() && val instanceof Map<?,?> m) {
1505         if (isAbsentOptionalMap(m, targetType))
1506            return null;  // Let MarshallingSession convert null to Optional.of(Optional.empty())
1507         return collapseOptionalValue(m.get("value"), targetType.getElementType());
1508      }
1509      if (targetType.isBean() && val instanceof Map<?,?> m2) {
1510         collapseOptionalWrappers(m2, targetType);
1511         return m2;
1512      }
1513      return val;
1514   }
1515
1516   @SuppressWarnings({
1517      "unchecked" // (Map<String,Object>) cast when creating nested maps at path segments
1518   })
1519   private static void setByPath(Map<String,Object> target, String path, Object value) throws ParseException {
1520      var parts = path.split("\\.");
1521      if (parts.length == 1) {
1522         target.put(path, value);
1523         return;
1524      }
1525      Object current = target;
1526      for (int i = 0; i < parts.length - 1; i++) {
1527         var key = parts[i];
1528         var currentMap = (Map<?,?>) current;
1529         current = ((Map<String,Object>)currentMap).computeIfAbsent(key, k -> new JsonMap());
1530      }
1531      ((Map<String,Object>)current).put(parts[parts.length - 1], value);
1532   }
1533
1534   /** Wraps Map in JsonMap when target expects JsonMap (e.g. bean with constructor A(JsonMap)). */
1535   private static Object prepareMapForBean(Object value, ClassMeta<?> type) {
1536      if (value == null)
1537         return value;
1538      if (value instanceof JsonMap)
1539         return value;
1540      if (value instanceof Map<?, ?> m && !type.isMap())
1541         return new JsonMap(m);
1542      return value;
1543   }
1544
1545   /** Unwraps ValueHolder map {value: X} to X when target is scalar; else converts via MarshallingSession. */
1546   private Object unwrapValueHolder(Object row, ClassMeta<?> targetType) throws ParseException {
1547      if (!(row instanceof Map<?, ?> m))
1548         return convertToType(row, targetType);
1549      // Unwrap {value: X} when target is scalar (or Object) - allow maps with extra keys (e.g. _type)
1550      if (m.containsKey("value") && (targetType.isObject()
1551         || (!targetType.isMap() && !targetType.isCollection() && !targetType.isArray()))) {
1552         var v = m.get("value");
1553         if (targetType.isObject())
1554            return v;
1555         return convertToType(prepareMapForBean(v, targetType), targetType);
1556      }
1557      return convertToType(prepareMapForBean(row, targetType), targetType);
1558   }
1559
1560   private Object toArray(List<Object> values, ClassMeta<?> componentType) throws ParseException {
1561      var ct = componentType.inner();
1562      var arr = Array.newInstance(ct, values.size());
1563      for (int i = 0; i < values.size(); i++)
1564         Array.set(arr, i, convertToType(values.get(i), componentType));
1565      return arr;
1566   }
1567
1568   @SuppressWarnings({
1569      "unchecked" // Collection.newInstance() returns raw type; cast to Collection<Object> for add
1570   })
1571   private Object toCollection(List<Object> values, ClassMeta<?> collectionType) throws ParseException {
1572      var elemType = collectionType.getElementType();
1573      Collection<Object> result;
1574      try {
1575         result = collectionType.canCreateNewInstance() ? (Collection<Object>)collectionType.newInstance() : null;
1576      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") Exception ignored) {
1577         result = null;
1578      }
1579      if (result == null)
1580         result = new ArrayList<>();
1581      for (var v : values)
1582         result.add((Object)convertToType(v, elemType));
1583      return result;
1584   }
1585}
1586
